selected_goal.title: Migrate the snake game from terminal/curses to a standalone pygame GUI window
selected_goal.rationale: The user's original goal is to build a snake game. The default product fit for a Python snake game is a standalone GUI window, not a terminal/curses interface. Pygame provides the expected visual experience with independent window, proper event handling, and standard game loop.
acceptance_criteria: Running '.venv/bin/python main.py' opens a pygame window instead of launching in the terminal
The snake moves continuously in the window and changes direction with arrow keys
A red square appears as food and the snake grows when eating it
The current score is displayed on the window (e.g., as a title or overlay)
When the snake hits a wall or itself, a 'Game Over' screen appears showing the final score and offering restart (R key) and quit (Q key) options
The game runs without any terminal dependencies (no curses module import)

designed_tasks.description: Rewrite main.py to use pygame: create a standalone window with snake movement (arrow keys), red food square, score display (title/overlay), and game-over screen with restart (R) and quit (Q) options. Ensure no curses module import.
acceptance_criteria: Running '.venv/bin/python main.py' opens a pygame window instead of launching in the terminal
The snake moves continuously in the window and changes direction with arrow keys
A red square appears as food and the snake grows when eating it
The current score is displayed on the window (e.g., as a title or overlay)
When the snake hits a wall or itself, a 'Game Over' screen appears showing the final score and offering restart (R key) and quit (Q key) options
The game runs without any terminal dependencies (no curses module import)
risk_notes: Pygame may not be installed in the virtual environment; task must install pygame via pip before execution
The game requires a graphical display environment; may not work in a headless context
